title: Test shared semantic memory integration end to end
description: Add and update automated tests covering backend initialization, semantic CRUD/search, ingestion and deduplication, runner env injection, planner spec ingestion, onboarding bootstrap, and CLI-visible behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover `init_db()` idempotency and WAL-ready bootstrap behavior.
Tests cover `add_entry()` plus `search()` round-trip behavior, including all-namespace search output.
Tests cover `ingest_file()` chunking and deduplication behavior.
Tests cover env injection for both runner backends, including standalone bead behavior.
Tests cover planner spec ingestion including non-fatal failure handling.
Tests cover onboarding overwrite/bootstrap behavior as needed for the new init flow.
The relevant test suite passes with `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`.

handoff_summary.completed: Ran all 4 new test files (test_memory_backend.py: 48 tests, test_cli_memory.py: 17 tests, test_runner_memory_env.py: 18 tests, test_memory_templates.py: 22 tests) — 104/104 pass. Extended tests in test_planner.py (8 tests including 4 new spec ingestion tests) and test_onboarding_scaffold.py (38 tests including 5 new memory bootstrap tests) — all pass. Key regression suite (test_cli_bead, test_cli_merge, test_cli_run, test_cli_telemetry, test_scheduler_*, test_storage, test_prompts) — 202 passed, 0 failures.
handoff_summary.risks: None. All tests green. The single blocker (sqlite-vec KNN syntax) was resolved by the corrective bead.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used deterministic unit-vector mocks (sqlite_vec.serialize_float32 keyed by hash(text) % 384) to test semantic search without ONNX model download. CLI ingest tests patched ingest_file() at the command module level. Runner env tests patched build_worker_prompt() to avoid requiring templates on disk.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: init_db: 7 tests (idempotency, WAL mode, parent dir, download_model call) — all pass. add_entry + search round-trip: 10 tests — all pass after KNN fix. delete_entry: 3 tests — all pass. stats: 4 tests — all pass. ingest_file: 5 tests (dedup, idempotency, namespace/source) — all pass after KNN fix. chunk functions (_chunk_json, _chunk_csv, _chunk_file, _split_if_large): 18 tests — all pass. CLI subcommands: 17 tests — all pass. Runner env injection: 18 tests — all pass. Template verification: 22 tests — all pass. Planner spec ingestion: 4 tests — all pass. Onboarding bootstrap: 5 tests — all pass.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: None remaining. The sqlite-vec 0.1.9 KNN syntax incompatibility was the sole blocker and is now fixed.

execution_history.summary: Added 114 new tests covering all required acceptance criteria. 98 pass immediately; 16 fail due to a genuine implementation defect in memory.py:search() where LIMIT ? is not recognized by sqlite-vec 0.1.9 + SQLite 3.51.3 (Python 3.14). All pre-existing 1169 tests continue to pass (no regressions).
Created corrective bead B-267580f8-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-267580f8-corrective completed

All 104 new memory tests now pass after the corrective bead fixed memory.py:search() (sqlite-vec KNN syntax: LIMIT ? → AND k = ?). The 4 expected test files plus planner/onboarding extensions all pass. 202 existing tests show no regressions.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `templates/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing or running any tests, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable testing knowledge** — patterns, pitfalls, or edge cases that would benefit future testers of this codebase.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**

worker_prompt_context.title: Test shared semantic memory integration end to end
worker_prompt_context.description: Add and update automated tests covering backend initialization, semantic CRUD/search, ingestion and deduplication, runner env injection, planner spec ingestion, onboarding bootstrap, and CLI-visible behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover `init_db()` idempotency and WAL-ready bootstrap behavior.
Tests cover `add_entry()` plus `search()` round-trip behavior, including all-namespace search output.
Tests cover `ingest_file()` chunking and deduplication behavior.
Tests cover env injection for both runner backends, including standalone bead behavior.
Tests cover planner spec ingestion including non-fatal failure handling.
Tests cover onboarding overwrite/bootstrap behavior as needed for the new init flow.
The relevant test suite passes with `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`.
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: None — test files only. No overlap with any other bead's expected files.

handoff_summary.completed: Wrote 4 new test files and extended 2 existing test files: test_memory_backend.py (48 tests covering init_db, add_entry, search, delete_entry, stats, ingest_file, _chunk_json, _chunk_csv, _chunk_file dispatch, _split_if_large), test_cli_memory.py (17 tests covering all 6 memory subcommands and error paths), test_runner_memory_env.py (18 tests covering _find_project_root, _resolve_takt_cmd, CodexAgentRunner env injection, ClaudeCodeAgentRunner env injection, standalone bead global fallback, caller override), test_memory_templates.py (22 tests verifying all 5 template memory sections). Added 4 spec ingestion tests to test_planner.py and 5 memory bootstrap tests to test_onboarding_scaffold.py.
handoff_summary.remaining: Developer fix required: memory.py:search() must replace 'LIMIT ?' with 'AND k = ?' in the sqlite-vec KNN query WHERE clause. Once fixed, all 16 failing tests will pass.
handoff_summary.risks: The sqlite-vec LIMIT ? defect means search(), ingest_file() deduplication, and all CLI search commands fail at runtime in the current environment (Python 3.14 / SQLite 3.51.3 / sqlite-vec 0.1.9). This is not a test artifact — real agent memory operations would fail.
handoff_summary.next_action: Developer must fix memory.py:search() — replace 'LIMIT ?' with 'AND k = ?' in the sqlite-vec KNN query. The WHERE clause should read: 'WHERE v.embedding MATCH ? AND k = ? ORDER BY v.distance'. This one-line change will make all 16 failing tests pass and make the memory subsystem functional in the Python 3.14 / SQLite 3.51.3 environment.
handoff_summary.block_reason: memory.py:search() fails at runtime with 'sqlite3.OperationalError: A LIMIT or k = ? constraint is required on vec0 knn queries.' on Python 3.14 / sqlite-vec 0.1.9 / SQLite 3.51.3. The implementation uses 'LIMIT ?' SQL parameter binding but this version of sqlite-vec requires 'AND k = ?' in the WHERE clause instead. All 16 search-dependent tests fail. Fix: in memory.py around line 393, change 'WHERE v.embedding MATCH ? ORDER BY v.distance LIMIT ?' to 'WHERE v.embedding MATCH ? AND k = ? ORDER BY v.distance' (same parameter tuple, just reordered semantics).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — test files only. No overlap with any other bead's expected files.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Mocked _embed() to return deterministic unit-vector blobs (sqlite_vec.serialize_float32) keyed by hash(text) % 384 — avoids ONNX model download while producing meaningful distance relationships. Mocked _download_model() to no-op throughout. CLI ingest tests patched ingest_file() at the command module level (not memory module) to bypass the KNN bug cleanly while still testing CLI dispatch logic. Runner env tests patched build_worker_prompt() to avoid requiring templates on disk.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: init_db: idempotency, WAL mode, parent dir creation, download_model called (7 tests all pass). add_entry: UUID returned (1 pass). search: 10 tests all fail due to KNN bug. delete_entry: raises ValueError for unknown ID (pass), removes from DB (fail — uses search), removes vector row (pass). stats: 4 tests all pass. ingest_file: 5 tests all fail — ingest calls search() for dedup. chunk functions: 18 tests all pass. CLI subcommands: 17 tests all pass (ingest patched to bypass KNN). Runner env: 18 tests all pass. Template verification: 22 tests all pass. Planner ingestion: 4 tests all pass. Onboarding bootstrap: 5 tests all pass.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: sqlite-vec 0.1.9 KNN query syntax incompatibility with Python 3.14 / SQLite 3.51.3 is the single blocker. The 16 failing tests will pass immediately after the LIMIT ? → AND k = ? fix. No other known issues in the test suite.

last_agent_result.summary: All 104 new memory tests now pass after the corrective bead fixed memory.py:search() (sqlite-vec KNN syntax: LIMIT ? → AND k = ?). The 4 expected test files plus planner/onboarding extensions all pass. 202 existing tests show no regressions.
